title: negundo nuttallii
intro: Negundo nuttallii, also known as Black Cottonwood or Western Black Cottonwood, is a deciduous tree native to the Pacific coast of North America, belonging to the family Salicaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds that help to soothe and heal wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. The health benefits of Negundo nuttallii include its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce inflammation, and act as an expectorant to relieve coughs and congestion. Herbal preparations of Negundo nuttallii include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the bark, leaves, or roots in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
